channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: iPad mini 7 specs reveal 5 potentially annoying limitations
item.description: The iPad mini 7 is now official – and the tablet's specs reveal that Apple has cut a few corners to keep its price tag low.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Nuclear Power Revolutionizes Energy Supply in Data Centers - A New Era of Clean Energy?
item.description: Nuclear power is gaining traction in data centers, with companies like Oracle and Microsoft investigating its potential. Micro nuclear reactors are set to provide clean energy, and Westinghouse's eVinci microreactor has reached a significant milestone.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How Libya and Tuvalu are cashing in on their top-level domain names
item.description: Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it’s not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Army modernization from the technology point of view
item.description: "We're working with our development teams and we're working with testing. And then we have a policy that covers all of that," said Young Bang. The post Army modernization from the technology point of view first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Derace Lauderdale, Federal News Network

item.title: AI makes edge computing more relevant to CIOs
item.description: Analysts predict the incoming phase of enterprise AI will herald agentic systems that require minimal human intervention, with 75% of CIOs increasing their AI budgets during this year alone, according to a recent report from Gartner.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cómo gestionar los programas de IA generativa: gobernanza, educación, regulación
item.description: La inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa ha crecido con gran rapidez, atrayendo inversiones y despertando el interés y la creatividad de los desarrolladores. Las empresas ven en la IA generativa una vía para crear servicios innovadores y revolucionar sus sectores.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: WasmGC and the future of front-end Java development
item.description: JavaScript is the clear choice for front-end development today, but there is a longstanding push to be able to use Java on the front end. Among other things, Java brings a vast ecosystem of libraries, frameworks, and application code, which can be exposed via its APIs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: SSA Aims to Become Most Accessible Agency, Reinvigorate Services for Americans
item.description: The Social Security Administration is revamping its services to better serve 70 million Americans annually. With the lowest staffing levels in over 50 years, SSA aims to digitize and simplify processes to ensure all customers can interact with the agency through their preferred channel.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google endorses California GOP Rep. Issa’s bill to reveal funders backing lawsuits
item.description: The bill, introduced last week would require plaintiffs to reveal any investors who could make money based on the outcome of a case. It would also require parties to disclose the financing agreement between investors and those involved.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Countries that have cashed in on their top-level domain names
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Anguilla is raking in extra revenue from registration fees for its .ai web domain thanks to the artificial intelligence boom, but it's not the only place cashing in on demand for websites with distinctive address endings.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: How to choose a flexible unified SASE solution
item.description: SASE is at the top of all CIOs’ minds, and for good reason. When deployed properly, SASE, which combines SD-WAN with cloud-delivered security via SSE, enables all users and devices, regardless of location, to securely access an organization’s hybrid network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Corsair upgrades high-end gaming headset with Virtuoso Max Wireless
item.description: Reportedly priced at $329.99, the headset features active noise cancellation, RGB lighting accents and Dolby Atmos support
item.dc:creator: Gieson Cacho, The Mercury News

item.title: Tech consultant goes on trial in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The tech consultant charged in Cash App founder Bob Lee's stabbing death had no motive to kill him and in fact was forced to defend himself against Lee, who had become aggressive while on a multiday drug bender, lawyers for Nima Momeni said in opening statements Monday.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: Here’s what you need to do before and after your phone is stolen or lost
item.description: There are things you can do to make it less painful if your phone is stolen. Because some of these features are more technical in nature, people often overlook them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Excel basics: Get started with charts and sparklines
item.description: Microsoft Excel offers a plethora of tools for representing your data visually. The most basic of these — and arguably the most useful — is the humble chart. But it’s not always easy to know where to begin with charts. We’re here to help.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
